1439	/-- **First-projection bridge to `awayInclLeft`** (`lem:gr_baseChange_bridge_left`): the
1440	global-sections base-change map of the first projection `p^{IJ}_{IJK} : V_IJK ⟶ U^I_J`,
1441	transported through the affine identifications, is the ring homomorphism
1442	`awayInclLeft (P^I_J) (P^I_K)`. Project-local. -/
1443	lemma baseChange_bridge_left (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
1444	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
1445	    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv ≫
1446	        Scheme.Hom.appTop
1447	          (Y := Spec (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ))))
1448	          (Limits.pullback.fst (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ) (chartIncl d r I K hI hK))
1449	      = CommRingCat.ofHom (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)) ≫
1450	        tripleOverlapSections d r I J K hI hJ hK := by
1451	  have hfst : (awayPullbackIso (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).inv ≫
1452	        Limits.pullback.fst (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ) (chartIncl d r I K hI hK)
1453	      = Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
1454	          (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))) :=
1455	    awayPullbackIso_inv_fst _ _
1456	  have hp : Limits.pullback.fst (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ) (chartIncl d r I K hI hK)
1457	      = (awayPullbackIso (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).hom ≫
1458	        Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
1459	          (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))) :=
1460	    (Iso.inv_comp_eq _).mp hfst
1461	  rw [hp, Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop]
1462	  -- term-mode reassociation (positional `rw [← Category.assoc]` misses the comp node: the
1463	  -- middle-object representation differs across the `pullback (chartIncl …)` defeq)
1464	  exact (Category.assoc _ _ _).symm.trans ((congrArg
1465	    (· ≫ Scheme.Hom.appTop
1466	      (awayPullbackIso (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).hom)
1467	    (baseChange_bridge_gammaSpec (CommRingCat.ofHom
1468	      (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))))).trans
1469	    (Category.assoc _ _ _))

1482	        tripleOverlapSections d r I J K hI hJ hK := by

1484	        Limits.pullback.snd (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ) (chartIncl d r I K hI hK)

1502	/-- **Triple-transition bridge to `Θ_{IJ}`** (`lem:gr_baseChange_bridge_transition`): the
1503	global-sections base-change map of the composite `t'_{IJK} ≫ p^{JK}_{JKI} : V_IJK ⟶ U^J_K`,
1504	transported through the affine identifications, is the localised cocycle homomorphism
1505	`Θ_{IJ} ∘ awayInclRight (P^J_I) (P^J_K)` — exactly the composite over which the matrix

1507	order-swap `awayMulCommEquiv` of `chartTransition'` is absorbed by

1509	lemma baseChange_bridge_transition (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
1510	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
1511	    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))).inv ≫
1512	        Scheme.Hom.appTop
1513	          (Y := Spec (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r J K hJ hK))))
1514	          (chartTransition' d r I J K hI hJ hK ≫
1515	            Limits.pullback.fst (chartIncl d r J K hJ hK) (chartIncl d r J I hJ hI))
1516	      = CommRingCat.ofHom ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
1517	          (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK))) ≫

1520	        Limits.pullback.fst (chartIncl d r J K hJ hK) (chartIncl d r J I hJ hI)

1522	          (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r J K hJ hK) (minorDet d r J I hJ hI))) :=
1523	    awayPullbackIso_inv_fst _ _

1525	        Limits.pullback.fst (chartIncl d r J K hJ hK) (chartIncl d r J I hJ hI)
1526	      = (awayPullbackIso (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).hom ≫
1527	        Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
1528	          (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))) := by
1529	    rw [chartTransition']
1530	    simp only [Category.assoc]
1531	    -- `erw` (defeq matching) to fire the fst-leg lemma through the `HasPullback` instance

1549	  refine (congrArg (fun m => (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away
1550	    (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))).inv ≫ Scheme.Hom.appTop m) hp).trans ?_
1551	  rw [Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop]
1552	  -- term-mode reassociation (see `baseChange_bridge_left`)
1553	  exact (Category.assoc _ _ _).symm.trans ((congrArg
1554	    (· ≫ Scheme.Hom.appTop
1555	      (awayPullbackIso (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).hom)
1556	    (baseChange_bridge_gammaSpec (CommRingCat.ofHom ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
1557	      (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))))).trans
1558	    (Category.assoc _ _ _))

1560	/-- **Base-change bridge to the localised cocycle, matrix form** (`lem:gr_baseChange_bridge`):
1561	over the triple overlap `V_IJK` the three geometrically base-changed Cramer inverses satisfy
1562	the multiplicative cocycle. The three projection bridges rewrite each base-changed matrix as
1563	the σ-image (`tripleOverlapSections`) of the corresponding L1 matrix, and the matrix-level
1564	cocycle `bundleTransition_cocycle_matrix` transports along the ring homomorphism σ.
1565	Project-local — this is the (b)-step of the bundle cocycle. -/
1566	theorem baseChange_bridge (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
1567	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
1568	    (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop
1569	          (Y := Spec (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r J K hJ hK))))
1570	          (chartTransition' d r I J K hI hJ hK ≫
1571	            Limits.pullback.fst (chartIncl d r J K hJ hK) (chartIncl d r J I hJ hI)))).mapMatrix
1572	        ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away
1573	            (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix (universalMinorInv d r J K hJ hK))
1574	      * (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop
1575	            (Y := Spec (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ))))
1576	            (Limits.pullback.fst (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ)
1577	              (chartIncl d r I K hI hK)))).mapMatrix
1578	          ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away
1579	              (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ))
1580	      = (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.Hom.appTop
1581	            (Y := Spec (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I K hI hK))))
1582	            (Limits.pullback.snd (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ)
1583	              (chartIncl d r I K hI hK)))).mapMatrix
1584	          ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (Localization.Away
1585	              (minorDet d r I K hI hK)))).inv.hom.mapMatrix
1586	            (universalMinorInv d r I K hI hK)) := by
1587	  have hL := congrArg CommRingCat.Hom.hom (baseChange_bridge_left d r I J K hI hJ hK)
1588	  have hR := congrArg CommRingCat.Hom.hom (baseChange_bridge_right d r I J K hI hJ hK)
1589	  have hT := congrArg CommRingCat.Hom.hom (baseChange_bridge_transition d r I J K hI hJ hK)
1590	  simp only [CommRingCat.hom_comp, CommRingCat.hom_ofHom] at hL hR hT
1591	  -- collapse the iterated `mapMatrix`s into single `Matrix.map`s along composite ring homs,
1592	  -- rewrite the composites through the three bridges, and split off the σ-factor
1593	  simp only [RingHom.mapMatrix_apply, Matrix.map_map, ← RingHom.coe_comp, hL, hR, hT]
1594	  -- split off exactly the outer σ-layer of each factor, recombine the product under σ, and
1595	  -- close by the matrix cocycle L1; a `calc` keeps every sub-goal freshly elaborated (the
1596	  -- carrier representations `↥(of R)` vs `R` block positional `rw` on the simp-produced goal)
1597	  calc (universalMinorInv d r J K hJ hK).map
1598	          ⇑((CommRingCat.Hom.hom (tripleOverlapSections d r I J K hI hJ hK)).comp
1599	            ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
1600	              (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))) *
1601	        (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ).map
1602	          ⇑((CommRingCat.Hom.hom (tripleOverlapSections d r I J K hI hJ hK)).comp

972	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — relative-tensor whiskering preserves `J.W`
973	
974	The class `J.W` of abelian local isomorphisms is closed under right-whiskering by an
975	arbitrary presheaf in the **pointwise** monoidal structure on `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A` whenever `A` is
976	braided monoidal closed: this is Mathlib's `GrothendieckTopology.W.whiskerRight`
977	(Day reflection, `CategoryTheory/Sites/Monoidal.lean`).  Two gaps separate that statement
978	from `ztensor_whisker_localIso`:
979	
980	* `Ab` carries no (tensor) monoidal structure in Mathlib, and the `ModuleCat` monoidal
981	  structure insists that ring and modules live in the same universe.  We therefore work in
982	  `ModuleCat.{u} (ULift.{u} ℤ)` and transport `J.W` along the carrier-preserving
983	  equivalence `modToAb` (an equivalence is a left adjoint in both directions, hence
984	  preserves sheafification both ways — `W_whiskerRight_modToAb_iff`).
985	* the morphism in `ztensor_whisker_localIso` is the *relative*-tensor whiskering
986	  `f ▷ R` (over `𝒪_X`), not the `ℤ`-tensor one.  The coequalizer presentation
987	  `relativeTensorCoequalizerIso` exhibits its underlying abelian map as the map induced on
988	  coequalizers by the two `ℤ`-tensor whiskered rows (`domWhisker`, `tripWhisker`); abelian
989	  sheafification preserves the coequalizers and inverts the rows, hence inverts the induced
990	  map (`GrothendieckTopology.W_iff`).
991	-/
992	
993	section ZTensorWhisker
994	
995	open TensorProduct
996	
997	/-- Promote an additive homomorphism of abelian groups to a `ULift ℤ`-linear map (any
998	additive map of abelian groups is `ℤ`-linear, and the `ULift ℤ`-action is the `ℤ`-action). -/
999	private def toULiftIntLinearMap {M N : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [AddCommGroup N]
1000	    (φ : M →+ N) : M →ₗ[ULift.{u} ℤ] N where
1001	  toFun := φ
1002	  map_add' := φ.map_add
1003	  map_smul' c x := by
1004	    change φ (c.down • x) = c.down • φ x
1005	    exact map_zsmul φ c.down x
1006	
1007	@[simp] private lemma toULiftIntLinearMap_apply {M N : Type u} [AddCommGroup M]
1008	    [AddCommGroup N] (φ : M →+ N) (x : M) : toULiftIntLinearMap φ x = φ x := rfl
1009	
1010	/-- The `ℤ`- and `ULift ℤ`-actions on abelian groups are tensor-compatible. -/
1011	private instance compatibleSMul_int_uliftInt (M N : Type u) [AddCommGroup M]
1012	    [AddCommGroup N] : CompatibleSMul ℤ (ULift.{u} ℤ) M N :=
1013	  ⟨fun c m n => smul_tmul c.down m n⟩
1014	
1015	/-- The relative tensor product over `ULift ℤ` of two abelian groups agrees with their
1016	`ℤ`-tensor product (`TensorProduct.equivOfCompatibleSMul`); both directions send an
1017	elementary tensor `m ⊗ₜ n` to `m ⊗ₜ n`. -/
1018	private noncomputable def uTensorEquiv (M N : Type u) [AddCommGroup M] [AddCommGroup N] :
1019	    (M ⊗[ULift.{u} ℤ] N) ≃ₗ[ℤ] (M ⊗[ℤ] N) :=
1020	  TensorProduct.equivOfCompatibleSMul ℤ (ULift.{u} ℤ) ℤ M N
1021	
1022	@[simp] private lemma uTensorEquiv_tmul (M N : Type u) [AddCommGroup M] [AddCommGroup N]
1023	    (m : M) (n : N) : uTensorEquiv M N (m ⊗ₜ n) = m ⊗ₜ n := rfl
1024	
1025	@[simp] private lemma uTensorEquiv_symm_tmul (M N : Type u) [AddCommGroup M]
1026	    [AddCommGroup N] (m : M) (n : N) : (uTensorEquiv M N).symm (m ⊗ₜ n) = m ⊗ₜ n := rfl
1027	
1028	/-- The triple-tensor variant of `uTensorEquiv`:
1029	`M ⊗[ULift ℤ] (S ⊗[ULift ℤ] N) ≃ M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)`, sending `m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)` to itself. -/
1030	private noncomputable def uTripleEquiv (M S N : Type u) [AddCommGroup M] [AddCommGroup S]
1031	    [AddCommGroup N] :
1032	    (M ⊗[ULift.{u} ℤ] (S ⊗[ULift.{u} ℤ] N)) ≃ₗ[ℤ] (M ⊗[ℤ] (S ⊗[ℤ] N)) :=
1033	  (uTensorEquiv M (S ⊗[ULift.{u} ℤ] N)) ≪≫ₗ
1034	    (TensorProduct.congr (LinearEquiv.refl ℤ M) (uTensorEquiv S N))
1035	
1036	@[simp] private lemma uTripleEquiv_tmul (M S N : Type u) [AddCommGroup M] [AddCommGroup S]
1037	    [AddCommGroup N] (m : M) (s : S) (n : N) :
1038	    uTripleEquiv M S N (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)) = m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n) := rfl
1039	
1040	@[simp] private lemma uTripleEquiv_symm_tmul (M S N : Type u) [AddCommGroup M]
1041	    [AddCommGroup S] [AddCommGroup N] (m : M) (s : S) (n : N) :
1042	    (uTripleEquiv M S N).symm (m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n)) = m ⊗ₜ (s ⊗ₜ n) := rfl
1043	
1044	/-- The presheaf of `ULift ℤ`-modules underlying a presheaf of `𝒪_X`-modules, with the
1045	syntactic `↥(P.obj U)` carriers of `objRestrict`.  This places the underlying abelian
1046	presheaf of `P` in a category (`Cᵒᵖ ⥤ ModuleCat (ULift ℤ)`) which Mathlib equips with a
1047	pointwise braided monoidal-closed structure, so that
1048	`GrothendieckTopology.W.whiskerRight` applies. -/
1049	private noncomputable def uModPresheaf (P : X.PresheafOfModules) :
1050	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ ModuleCat.{u} (ULift.{u} ℤ) where
1051	  obj U := ModuleCat.of (ULift.{u} ℤ) ↥(P.obj U)
1052	  map {U V} g := ModuleCat.ofHom (toULiftIntLinearMap (objRestrict P g).toAddMonoidHom)
1053	  map_id U := by
1054	    ext x
1055	    exact LinearMap.congr_fun (objRestrict_id P U) x
1056	  map_comp {U V W} g h := by
1057	    ext x
1058	    exact LinearMap.congr_fun (objRestrict_comp P g h) x
1059	
1060	/-- The presheaf of `ULift ℤ`-modules underlying the structure sheaf of `X`. -/
1061	private noncomputable def uModRingPresheaf (X : Scheme.{u}) :
1062	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ ModuleCat.{u} (ULift.{u} ℤ) where
1063	  obj U := ModuleCat.of (ULift.{u} ℤ) ↥(X.sheaf.obj.obj U)
1064	  map {U V} g := ModuleCat.ofHom
1065	    (toULiftIntLinearMap (X.sheaf.obj.map g).hom.toAddMonoidHom)
1066	  map_id U := by
1067	    ext s
1068	    change (X.sheaf.obj.map (𝟙 U)).hom s = s
1069	    rw [CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]
1070	    rfl
1071	  map_comp {U V W} g h := by
1072	    ext s
1073	    change (X.sheaf.obj.map (g ≫ h)).hom s
1074	      = (X.sheaf.obj.map h).hom ((X.sheaf.obj.map g).hom s)
1075	    rw [CategoryTheory.Functor.map_comp]
1076	    rfl
1077	
1078	/-- The morphism of `ULift ℤ`-module presheaves underlying a morphism of presheaves of
1079	modules. -/
1080	private noncomputable def uModHom {P Q : X.PresheafOfModules} (f : P ⟶ Q) :
1081	    uModPresheaf P ⟶ uModPresheaf Q where
1082	  app U := ModuleCat.ofHom (toULiftIntLinearMap (f.app U).hom.toAddMonoidHom)
1083	  naturality {U V} g := by
1084	    ext x
1085	    exact PresheafOfModules.naturality_apply f g x
1086	
1087	/-- The carrier-preserving equivalence from `ULift ℤ`-modules to abelian groups:
1088	restriction of scalars along `ℤ ≅ ULift ℤ` followed by the standard equivalence
1089	`ModuleCat ℤ ≌ Ab`. -/
1090	private noncomputable def modToAb : ModuleCat.{u} (ULift.{u} ℤ) ⥤ Ab.{u} :=
1091	  ModuleCat.restrictScalars (ULift.ringEquiv.symm : ℤ ≃+* ULift.{u} ℤ).toRingHom ⋙
1092	    forget₂ (ModuleCat.{u} ℤ) AddCommGrpCat.{u}
1093	
1094	private instance : modToAb.{u}.IsEquivalence := by
1095	  unfold modToAb
1096	  infer_instance
1097	
1098	@[simp] private lemma modToAb_map_apply {M N : ModuleCat.{u} (ULift.{u} ℤ)} (ψ : M ⟶ N)
1099	    (x : M) : (ConcreteCategory.hom (modToAb.map ψ)) x = ψ.hom x := rfl
1100	
1101	/-- **`J.W` transfers along `modToAb`** (both directions).  The equivalence `modToAb` is a
1102	left adjoint in both directions, hence preserves sheafification both ways
1103	(`Sheaf.preservesSheafification_of_adjunction`). -/
1104	private lemma W_whiskerRight_modToAb_iff {C : Type u} [SmallCategory C]
1105	    (J : GrothendieckTopology C) {F G : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ ModuleCat.{u} (ULift.{u} ℤ)} (ψ : F ⟶ G) :
1106	    J.W (Functor.whiskerRight ψ modToAb.{u}) ↔ J.W ψ := by
1107	  haveI h₁ : J.PreservesSheafification modToAb.{u} :=
1108	    Sheaf.preservesSheafification_of_adjunction J modToAb.{u}.asEquivalence.toAdjunction
1109	  haveI h₂ : J.PreservesSheafification modToAb.{u}.asEquivalence.inverse :=
1110	    Sheaf.preservesSheafification_of_adjunction J modToAb.{u}.asEquivalence.symm.toAdjunction

1301	  | tmul m y =>
1302	    induction y using TensorProduct.induction_on with
1303	    | zero =>
1304	      exact (congrArg _ (TensorProduct.tmul_zero _ m)).trans
1305	        (((map_zero _).trans (map_zero _).symm).trans
1306	          (congrArg _ (TensorProduct.tmul_zero _ m)).symm)
1307	    | tmul s n =>
1308	      have t1 : (AddCommGrpCat.Hom.hom ((relTensorActL P R).app U))
1309	          (m ⊗ₜ[ℤ] (s ⊗ₜ[ℤ] n)) = (s • m) ⊗ₜ[ℤ] n := rfl
1310	      have t2 : (AddCommGrpCat.Hom.hom ((domWhisker f R).app U)) ((s • m) ⊗ₜ[ℤ] n)

1313	          (m ⊗ₜ[ℤ] (s ⊗ₜ[ℤ] n))

1322	        t2).trans (congrArg (fun w => w ⊗ₜ[ℤ] n) key)).trans

1331	    exact congrArg₂ (fun x y => x + y) ha hb

1399	/-- **A ℤ-whiskered stalkwise isomorphism is a local isomorphism** (`lem:snap_ztensor_whisker_localIso`).
1400	Let `f : P ⟶ Q` be a morphism of presheaves of `𝒪_X`-modules such that the underlying
1401	abelian-presheaf morphism `(toPresheaf _).map f` lies in the weak-equivalence class `J.W`
1402	of the opens topology on `X` (i.e., `f` is a stalkwise isomorphism of abelian-group
1403	presheaves). Then for any presheaf of modules `R`, the underlying abelian morphism of the
1404	right-whiskered map `f ▷ R : P ⊗_p R ⟶ Q ⊗_p R` (in the presheaf monoidal structure
1405	`PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory`) is again a stalkwise isomorphism, hence lies in `J.W`.
1406	
1407	Proof route (actual — NOT the stalk route): present the underlying abelian presheaf of
1408	`P ⊗_p R` as the coequalizer of the two `𝒪`-action rows (`relativeTensorCoequalizerIso`);
1409	abelian sheafification `a = presheafToSheaf J Ab` is a left adjoint, so it preserves this
1410	coequalizer.  The whiskered rows `tripWhisker f R` / `domWhisker f R` lie in `J.W` by
1411	`W_tripWhisker` / `W_domWhisker` (the ULift/`W.whiskerRight` transfer at
1412	`ModuleCat (ULift ℤ)`), so `a` inverts them; the induced map of coequalizer points —
1413	which is `a.map` of our morphism — is then an isomorphism, i.e. the morphism lies in
1414	`J.W` by `GrothendieckTopology.W_iff`. -/
1415	lemma ztensor_whisker_localIso {P Q : X.PresheafOfModules}
1416	    (f : P ⟶ Q)
1417	    (hf : (opensTopology X).W ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map f))
1418	    (R : X.PresheafOfModules) :
1419	    (opensTopology X).W
1420	      ((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj).map
1421	        (MonoidalCategory.whiskerRight (C := MonoidalPresheaf X) f R)) := by
1422	  -- Apply the abelian sheafification functor `a` to the coequalizer presentations of
1423	  -- `P ⊗_p R` and `Q ⊗_p R` (`relativeTensorCoequalizerIso`); the whiskered rows
1424	  -- `tripWhisker`/`domWhisker` become isomorphisms (they lie in `J.W`), so the induced
1425	  -- map of coequalizer points — which is `a.map` of our morphism — is an isomorphism.
1426	  have hWdom : (opensTopology X).W (domWhisker f R) := W_domWhisker f hf R
1427	  have hWtrip : (opensTopology X).W (tripWhisker f R) := W_tripWhisker f hf R
1428	  rw [GrothendieckTopology.W_iff]
1429	  set a := presheafToSheaf (opensTopology X) Ab.{u} with ha
1430	  have hcP := Limits.isColimitOfPreserves a (relativeTensorCoequalizerIso P R)
1431	  have hcQ := Limits.isColimitOfPreserves a (relativeTensorCoequalizerIso Q R)
1432	  -- the morphism of parallel pairs given by the whiskered rows
1433	  let β : Limits.parallelPair (relTensorActL P R) (relTensorActR P R) ⟶
1434	      Limits.parallelPair (relTensorActL Q R) (relTensorActR Q R) :=
1435	    Limits.parallelPairHom (relTensorActL P R) (relTensorActR P R)
1436	      (relTensorActL Q R) (relTensorActR Q R) (tripWhisker f R) (domWhisker f R)
1437	      (actL_domWhisker f R) (actR_domWhisker f R)
1438	  have hβ : ∀ j, IsIso ((Functor.whiskerRight β a).app j) := by
1439	    rintro (_ | _)
1440	    · show IsIso (a.map (β.app Limits.WalkingParallelPair.zero))
1441	      rw [show β.app Limits.WalkingParallelPair.zero = tripWhisker f R from
1442	        Limits.parallelPairHom_app_zero ..]
1443	      exact ((opensTopology X).W_iff _).mp hWtrip
1444	    · show IsIso (a.map (β.app Limits.WalkingParallelPair.one))
1445	      rw [show β.app Limits.WalkingParallelPair.one = domWhisker f R from
1446	        Limits.parallelPairHom_app_one ..]
1447	      exact ((opensTopology X).W_iff _).mp hWdom
1448	  haveI : IsIso (Functor.whiskerRight β a) :=
1449	    NatIso.isIso_of_isIso_app _
1450	  -- the induced map of cocone points is `a.map` of our morphism …

1777	/-- The tensor-power comparison isomorphism `L^⊗m ⊗ L^⊗m' ≅ L^⊗(m+m')` for the sheaf tensor
1778	power `tensorPow` (`lem:sheafTensorPow_add`, [Stacks, Tag 01CU]).

1780	Proof by induction on `m`: the base case `m = 0` uses the left-unitor `tensorObjUnitIso`
1781	(already in file, axiom-clean); the inductive step uses `tensorObjAssoc` (above), the braiding
1782	`tensorBraiding` (in file), and sheaf-level right-whiskering of the inductive hypothesis.

1784	checklist. -/
1785	noncomputable def tensorPowAdd (L : X.Modules) (m m' : ℕ) :
1786	    tensorObj (tensorPow L m) (tensorPow L m') ≅ tensorPow L (m + m') :=
1787	  match m with
1788	  | 0 =>
1789	    -- Base case: the left unitor, reindexed along `0 + m' = m'`.
1790	    tensorObjUnitIso (tensorPow L m') ≪≫
1791	      eqToIso (congrArg (tensorPow L) (Nat.zero_add m').symm)
1792	  | (k + 1) =>
1793	    -- (a) associator, (b) braiding under the left factor, (c) inverse associator,
1794	    -- (d) inductive hypothesis whiskered by `L` on the right, (e) reindexing.
1795	    tensorObjAssoc (tensorPow L k) L (tensorPow L m') ≪≫
1796	      tensorObjWhiskerLeftIso (tensorPow L k) (tensorBraiding L (tensorPow L m')) ≪≫
1797	      (tensorObjAssoc (tensorPow L k) (tensorPow L m') L).symm ≪≫
1798	      tensorObjWhiskerRightIso (tensorPowAdd L k m') L ≪≫
1799	      eqToIso (congrArg (tensorPow L) (Nat.succ_add k m').symm)

2223	/-- The **tautological quotient** `u : O^r ↠ U` (`def:tautological_quotient`): the global
2224	surjection assembled from the chart quotients `u^I` (`chartQuotientMap`), compatible with
2225	the bundle gluing of `universalQuotient`. Since `universalQuotient` is the descent
2226	equalizer of pushforwards, the morphism is `glueLift` of the per-chart adjoint
2227	transposes (`tautologicalQuotientComponent`); the equalizing condition — the chart
2228	compatibility `g_{IJ} ∘ f_{IJ}^* u^I = (t_{IJ} ≫ f_{JI})^* u^J`, whose matrix content is
2229	`X^J = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I` (`universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap` / `imageMatrix`) — is
2230	`tautologicalQuotient_overlap`, transposed through the adjunction by
2231	`tautologicalQuotientComponent_transpose`. -/
2232	noncomputable def tautologicalQuotient (d r : ℕ) :
2233	    SheafOfModules.free (R := (scheme d r).ringCatSheaf) (Fin r) ⟶ universalQuotient d r :=
2234	  Scheme.Modules.glueLift (theGlueData d r)
2235	    (fun I => SheafOfModules.free (R := ((theGlueData d r).U I).ringCatSheaf) (Fin d))
2236	    (bundleTransitionData d r)
2237	    (fun I => bundleTransition_self d r I.1 I.2)
2238	    (fun I J K => bundleTransition_cocycle d r I J K)
2239	    (fun I => tautologicalQuotientComponent d r I)
2240	    (fun p => (tautologicalQuotientComponent_transpose d r p.1 p.2).mpr
2241	      (tautologicalQuotient_overlap d r p.1 p.2))

2243	/-! ## The functor of points and the universal property
2244	
2245	The Grassmannian functor sends a scheme `T` to the set of *equivalence classes* of
2246	rank-`d` quotients `q : O_T^r ↠ F` with `F` locally free of rank `d`. We encode a single
2247	such quotient as the structure `RankQuotient r d T`, the equivalence as `RankQuotient.Rel`
2248	(an isomorphism of the targets commuting with the quotient maps), and the functor's value
2249	at `T` as the quotient `Quotient (rqSetoid r d T)`. The pullback action `rqPullback`
2250	together with `pullbackFreeIso`/`pullback_isLocallyFreeOfRank` (and the fact that the
2251	pullback functor preserves epimorphisms, being a left adjoint) realise functoriality.
2252	
2253	Because a sheaf of modules `F : T.Modules` is a large object, this quotient lives in
2254	`Type 1` (not `Type 0` as the original scaffold signature stated); the corrected universe
2255	is the only change to the pinned signature, and it is forced — the substantive content is
2256	unchanged. -/
2257	
2258	/-- A **rank-`d` quotient of `O_T^r`** on a scheme `T`: a sheaf of modules `F` on `T`,
2259	locally free of rank `d`, together with an epimorphism `q : O_T^r ↠ F`. This is the
2260	unbundled datum whose equivalence classes form the value of the Grassmannian functor. -/
2261	structure RankQuotient (r d : ℕ) (T : Scheme.{0}) where
2262	  /-- The quotient sheaf. -/
2263	  F : T.Modules
2264	  /-- The quotient map out of the trivial rank-`r` bundle. -/
2265	  q : SheafOfModules.free (R := T.ringCatSheaf) (Fin r) ⟶ F
2266	  /-- The quotient map is an epimorphism (surjective). -/
2267	  epi : Epi q
2268	  /-- The quotient sheaf is locally free of rank `d`. -/
2269	  locFree : SheafOfModules.IsLocallyFreeOfRank F d
2270	
2271	/-- Two rank-`d` quotients are **equivalent** when there is an isomorphism of the targets
2272	commuting with the quotient maps (equivalently, when the kernels of the quotient maps
2273	coincide). -/
2274	def RankQuotient.Rel {r d : ℕ} {T : Scheme.{0}} (x y : RankQuotient r d T) : Prop :=
2275	  ∃ f : x.F ≅ y.F, x.q ≫ f.hom = y.q
2276	
2277	lemma RankQuotient.rel_refl {r d : ℕ} {T : Scheme.{0}} (x : RankQuotient r d T) : x.Rel x :=
2278	  ⟨Iso.refl _, Category.comp_id _⟩
2279	
2280	lemma RankQuotient.rel_symm {r d : ℕ} {T : Scheme.{0}} {x y : RankQuotient r d T}
2281	    (h : x.Rel y) : y.Rel x := by
2282	  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h
2283	  exact ⟨f.symm, by rw [Iso.symm_hom, Iso.comp_inv_eq]; exact hf.symm⟩
2284	
2285	lemma RankQuotient.rel_trans {r d : ℕ} {T : Scheme.{0}} {x y z : RankQuotient r d T}
2286	    (h1 : x.Rel y) (h2 : y.Rel z) : x.Rel z := by
2287	  obtain ⟨f, hf⟩ := h1; obtain ⟨g, hg⟩ := h2
2288	  -- term-mode (the `T.Modules` def-diamond blocks positional category `rw`)

2621	/-- **The chart loci cover `T`** (Nitsure §1, the Nakayama step): at each point `t`, the
2622	fibre `F ⊗ κ(t)` is a `d`-dimensional quotient of `κ(t)^r`, so some `d` of the standard

2628	`O_V`; epi-ness of `q` gives stalkwise surjectivity at `t`, so over the residue field
2629	some `d`-column minor of the presenting `d × r` matrix has nonzero determinant; shrink
2630	`V` to the basic open of that determinant, on which the matrix is invertible and hence
2631	the composite an isomorphism (`matrixToFreeIso`). -/

2854	/-- **The chart composite is an isomorphism over the whole chart locus** — the local
2855	inverses glue (separation + gluing halves of the sheaf condition).
2856	
2857	PROOF (the blueprint's stalk-wise route, `lem:isIso_pullback_isoLocus_map`): being an
2858	isomorphism of sheaves of modules is stalk-local. Each point of the iso-locus has an
2859	open neighbourhood `U` on which `φ` pulls back to an isomorphism; restriction along the
2860	open immersion `U.ι` preserves stalks (`Scheme.Modules.restrictStalkNatIso`), so the
2861	stalk of (the abelian presheaf of) `φ` is invertible at every point of the locus. The
2862	same stalk comparison for the locus inclusion shows the restriction of `φ` to the locus
2863	is stalkwise invertible, hence an isomorphism of abelian sheaves by the stalk criterion
2864	(`TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso`); `toPresheaf` reflects it back to

No structural error; but the depth should be flagged for the planner.

They compile because the sorries are inside the evidence of `RankQuotient`, not the type. The plan agent should be aware the `represents` structure's forward direction (`toFun`) uses a sorry-backed `tautologicalRankQuotient`.
